human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for facilities-integration scope, evidence sufficiency handling, safe authority boundaries, and required escalation behavior in execution-processing-and-fulfillment.

review_notes: All required scenario IDs are represented and pass in results.
All required abilities are validated and covered in functionality-map.
Public-facing research sources are mapped to evidence and escalation behavior for facilities lifecycle safety.
Source-driven source-conflict escalation blocks overconfident completion recommendations.

specialist_owned_evidence: The lane is explicitly bounded to facilities state progression, evidence sufficiency validation, and return-to-service escalation in data-center and cloud contexts.
Authority sources include facilities, thermal, cloud terminology, continuity, and safety references mapped to concrete decision stages.
Scenario set includes normal execution, boundary handoff, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence escalations, and complete-evidence refusal paths.
Manifest and readiness notes preserve `specification-based` runtime strategy with explicit promotion criteria for retained implementation.
notes: validated package is complete and boundary-safe for facilities integration execution support.
Promotion to retained implementation requires sustained governance signoff, low-incident operation, and explicit operational acceptance.
No final return-to-service claim is made without explicit approvals and synchronized evidence across DCIM, ticketing, and safety sources.
